mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 3% indicates rising inflation, prompting central banks to tighten monetary policy, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate and CPI signal a tightening monetary policy amid inflation, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, strong labor market conditions (PAYEMS growth) often precede economic overheating, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in late-cycle periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) reflects robust economic demand, which typically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate below 4% indicates a tight labor market, which often precedes inflationary pressures and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signal strong consumer demand, which typically leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During supply-side shocks, declining industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates economic disruption, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in supply-side shock periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
